abstract:
Driven by the surge in deep convolutional neural network (CNN) development, computer vision has witnessed transformative breakthroughs in image classification, semantic segmentation, object detection, and super-resolution. CNNs offer a powerful framework for autonomous feature learning and hierarchical expression, enabling the extraction of complex patterns directly from raw input through specialized training. As deep learning continues to evolve, CNN architectures have grown increasingly sophisticated and diverse, progressively marginalized traditional machine learning techniques. 

This paper provides an essential foundation by detailing the core components of CNNs—including input layers, convolution and pooling operations, activation functions, batch normalization, dropout techniques, and fully connected layers—alongside their specific functional roles. Building on this theoretical groundwork, we offer a modern survey of the research landscape, tracking the evolution of CNN models across critical domains such as image classification, object detection, and video prediction. Finally, we analyze the persistent challenges inherent in deep CNNs, review proposed solutions, and suggest promising avenues for future research in the field.
